name: Arimoclomol NPC phase 2/3 trial and open-label extension
summary: A 12-month randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled arimoclomol trial in Niemann-Pick disease type C followed by an open-label extension with longer-term clinical severity and safety follow-up.
population: Children, adolescents, and adults with molecularly confirmed Niemann-Pick disease type C, many receiving background miglustat

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, and coverage repair added the 48-month open-label extension source; this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support because they can show whether arimoclomol changes function, safety, durability, and regulatory status in Niemann-Pick disease type C. The evidence remains disease-specific and narrower than proof of broad aging or LEV-relevant proteostasis restoration.
